summary: The CheckRedirect doc comment must describe this CLI's actual cookie-jar redirect behavior, not the generic nonce-re-derivation boilerplate.
reason: The generated comment claimed CheckRedirect calls c.authHeader() to re-sign nonce-bound schemes (OAuth 1.0a/SigV4/Hawk) on each hop, but artistly is cookie-auth and the function never re-derives any header — it only caps redirects at 10 and relies on the cookie jar. A regen re-emits the misleading boilerplate; this guard says to correct it for cookie-auth CLIs.
validated_outcome: Comment now matches the implementation; go build ./... and go vet ./... pass.
